notes: Official repo has train.py and YouTube-VOS/DAVIS dataset instructions, but model is propagation/transformer rather than diffusion noise prediction; do not call it Diff-DPO without rewriting objective.
Official repo has train.py/configs; non-diffusion flow-guided model, so DPO can only be output-level preference/fine-tune style.
Official repo has train.py and YouTube-VOS config; transformer inpainting model, not diffusion noise-prediction.
Repo/project describes training-free optical-flow-guided diffusion inference; use as frozen baseline unless training code is later found.
Repo has train.py, dataset.py, config.yaml and says train on YouTube-VOS with accelerate; strongest direct diffusion adapter candidate, but training recommends 8x80GB.
Repo labels itself inference code/training-free personalized path and TODO says training code under preparation; not an adapter candidate now.
Repo now exposes training script for CogVideoX inpainting; viable but expensive and should be gated separately before any run.
Repo release note says code of model inference/preprocessing/gradio; no training entry found in README, so use frozen baseline only for now.
Paper/repo describes a two-stage remover, but current repo search did not expose train command; user explicitly said not to treat MiniMax as trainable.
Found paper/OpenReview entry but no reliable public training repo; related work only until code is available.
Repo provides datasets/checkpoints/inference scripts; no train.py in repo listing. Also task is language-driven VI, not our binary-mask BR protocol.
Search points to VideoComp as a compositional/video-text alignment or compositing dataset/task, not a directly trainable BR inpainting baseline for our protocol.
